name: Cagrilintide
subtitle: Long-Acting Amylin Receptor Agonist | Weight Loss & Diabetes

modifications: N-terminal lipidation with γ-glutamic acid spacer and C20 fatty diacid
Engineered mutations reduce β-sheet formation and fibril propensity

overview: Novel long-acting lipidated amylin analog functioning as dual amylin and calcitonin receptor agonist for weight management and type 2 diabetes. Phase 3 trials demonstrate 22.7% weight loss with CagriSema combination.
mechanism: Subcutaneous injection enables optimal bioavailability, targeting dual amylin and calcitonin receptors for satiety and metabolic regulation. Enhances insulin sensitivity and controls gastric emptying.
keyBenefits: FDA development candidate with extensive Phase 3 data
Superior weight loss in combination with semaglutide (22.7%)

items.name: Obesity (Monotherapy)
items.description: Phase 3 trials demonstrate significant weight loss.

items.name: Obesity (CagriSema Combination)
items.description: 22.7% weight loss with CagriSema combination, surpassing existing therapies.

items.name: Weight Loss in Diabetic Patients
items.description: 15.7% weight loss in diabetic patients with concurrent glycemic improvements.

items.name: Glycemic Control
items.description: 2.2% HbA1c reduction with CagriSema versus semaglutide alone.

items.name: Insulin Sensitivity
items.description: Amylin receptor activation enhances insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism.

items.name: Satiety Enhancement
items.description: Dual pathway satiety via amylin and calcitonin receptor activation.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection to abdomen, thigh, or upper arm. Pre-filled pen design when commercially available.

materials: Bacteriostatic water (standard BAC water acceptable for <30 days)
Optional: 0.6% acetic acid to adjust pH to ~4.0
steps: CRITICAL: Requires acidic pH (3.5-4.5) to prevent fibril formation
Reconstitute slowly down vial side; swirl gently (do not shake)
Solution must be clear—discard if cloudy or particulate
Store refrigerated (2-8°C); inspect before each injection
Allow 15-30 minutes to reach room temperature before injection

interactions.notes: CagriSema combination achieves enhanced weight loss through complementary GLP-1 and amylin pathways.
No known direct interactions; different mechanisms allow concurrent use.
Compounded GI side effects create substantial risk without specialist supervision.
Well-tolerated in Phase 2/3 trials with no pharmacokinetic interactions.
Clinical trials demonstrated safe concurrent use with complementary mechanisms.
Both are amylin agonists; combination provides no additional benefit and increases GI risks.
Delayed gastric emptying may affect absorption; space administration by 1 hour.

timeline.effects: Gastrointestinal adaptation; mild nausea possible during dose escalation
Early weight loss (2-5%); noticeable appetite reduction
Significant weight loss acceleration (10-15%); improved satiety signals
Peak efficacy (15-23% weight loss); sustained maintenance with continued therapy

common: Gastrointestinal effects (nausea, vomiting, diarrhea) during initial weeks
Anti-cagrilintide antibodies develop in 46-73% but do not affect efficacy
Only 57.3% achieved maximum 2.4mg dose in REDEFINE 1 trial
stopSigns: Pancreatitis signs (severe abdominal pain radiating to back)
Significant injection site reactions or abscess formation
contraindications: Not recommended in pregnancy or breastfeeding
Not yet commercially available (FDA approval expected Q1 2026)
monitoring: No clinically significant QT prolongation observed
Monitor for fibril formation in reconstituted solution (must remain clear)

warning: Standard bacteriostatic water acceptable short-term but may degrade at neutral pH; optimal stability requires pH ~4.0
bad: Fibril formation at improper pH—solution must remain clear

references.title: Coadministered Cagrilintide and Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity
references.keyFindings: Phase 3a REDEFINE 1 trial: 3,417 adults without diabetes. Estimated mean weight loss -20.4% with CagriSema vs -3.0% placebo at 68 weeks. GI adverse events in 79.6% of treatment group, mainly transient and mild-to-moderate.

references.title: Cagrilintide-Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity and Type 2 Diabetes
references.keyFindings: Phase 3a REDEFINE 2 trial: Adults with BMI ≥27, HbA1c 7-10%, and type 2 diabetes. Mean weight loss -13.7% with CagriSema vs -3.4% placebo at 68 weeks. Significant improvements in glycemic measures.

references.title: Efficacy and safety of co-administered once-weekly cagrilintide 2.4 mg with once-weekly semaglutide 2.4 mg in type 2 diabetes: a multicentre, randomised, double-blind, active-controlled, phase 2 trial
references.authors: Frias JP, Deenadayalan S, Erichsen L, Knop FK, Lingvay I, Machineni S, Mathieu C, Pedersen SD, Davies M
references.keyFindings: 32-week phase 2 trial at 17 US sites. CagriSema showed greater weight loss vs semaglutide or cagrilintide alone. HbA1c improvements significant. Well tolerated with predominantly GI adverse events.

latestResearch.title: CagriSema Reduces Blood Pressure in Adults With Overweight or Obesity: REDEFINE 1
latestResearch.summary: REDEFINE 1 post-hoc analysis: CagriSema showed clinically relevant BP reductions (systolic -10.9 vs -2.8 mmHg placebo; diastolic -5.4 vs -1.7 mmHg) across wide range of subgroups including resistant hypertension.

latestResearch.title: CagriSema drives weight loss in rats by reducing energy intake and preserving energy expenditure
latestResearch.summary: Mechanistic study demonstrating CagriSema drives weight loss primarily through reduced energy intake while preserving energy expenditure, differentiating it from caloric restriction alone.

quickStats.frequency: Once weekly, same day each week
quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: -20°C frozen; Reconstituted: 2-8°C refrigerated, use within 30 days
